issues_found.description: The plan says App becomes a layout route rendering <Outlet> instead of <Routes>. This is correct, but it fails to address a critical structural detail: the <Form {...colabForm}> wrapper in ColaboradorDetalhe (lines 593-846) only wraps Cards A-C. When restructuring into Tabs (phase-3), the Form wrapper must SPAN across tabs (so the Save button in PageHeader can submit it), but it should only contain FormField elements from the Geral tab. The plan's subtask-3-1 mentions 'colabForm is used by Cards A-C AND by the Save button in PageHeader -- form must span all tabs' which is correct, but the Form component must wrap the Tabs component, not be inside a single TabsContent. This is addressed in the plan's critical notes but should be more explicit in the execution instructions.
issues_found.fix_required: In subtask-3-1, explicitly state that <Form {...colabForm}> must wrap the entire <Tabs> component (not be inside TabsContent value='geral'). The Form wrapper currently at line 593 should be moved to wrap the Tabs, and the closing </Form> at line 846 should move to after the Tabs close. Cards E and D (which are outside the form) will be inside TabsContent components that happen to be inside Form but don't contain FormField components, which is fine.

issues_found.description: The plan shows main.tsx target as '<TooltipProvider><RouterProvider router={router}/></TooltipProvider>' but omits the Toaster component. Currently Toaster is a sibling of App inside HashRouter > TooltipProvider. After migration, Toaster should remain outside RouterProvider since it doesn't need router context. The plan doesn't mention Toaster placement.
issues_found.fix_required: In subtask-0-2, explicitly note that <Toaster /> should be placed as a sibling to <RouterProvider> (both inside TooltipProvider), e.g.: <TooltipProvider><RouterProvider .../><Toaster /></TooltipProvider>. This is minor since Toaster uses sonner (not router), but it should be documented to avoid confusion.

issues_found.description: The plan's subtask-0-1 mentions ErrorBoundary wrapping but doesn't specify how it migrates. Currently ErrorBoundary wraps <Routes> inside <main>. In the data router pattern, errorElement is typically defined on routes. The plan needs to decide: keep ErrorBoundary wrapping <Outlet> in AppLayout (simplest, preserves current behavior), or migrate to per-route errorElement (more work, no clear benefit).
issues_found.fix_required: In subtask-0-1, specify: keep <ErrorBoundary> wrapping <Outlet> inside <main> element of AppLayout. This preserves current behavior with zero risk. Do NOT use per-route errorElement -- that's over-engineering for this sprint.

issues_found.description: The plan mentions imports: 'import createHashRouter from react-router and RouterProvider from react-router/dom (or react-router)'. In the installed react-router v7.13.0, createHashRouter is exported from 'react-router-dom' (re-exported from react-router). RouterProvider is exported from 'react-router/dom' and re-exported by react-router-dom. The import source should be consistent: both from 'react-router-dom' for simplicity, matching existing import patterns in the codebase.
issues_found.fix_required: Use 'import { createHashRouter, RouterProvider } from "react-router-dom"' in App.tsx. This keeps imports consistent with existing codebase patterns (all other router imports use react-router-dom).

issues_found.description: The sexo default fix changes defaultValues.sexo from 'M' to '' (empty string). But z.enum(['M','F']) does NOT accept empty string -- the form will have a type mismatch at the default value level. The plan suggests 'as any' or a transform, but the cleanest approach is to use z.union([z.enum(['M','F']), z.literal('')]).transform() or simply z.string().refine(). The plan acknowledges this but doesn't give a definitive approach.
issues_found.fix_required: Use the simplest approach: keep colabSchema's sexo as z.enum(['M','F']) with the error message. For defaultValues, cast: sexo: '' as 'M' | 'F'. This is a standard react-hook-form pattern for 'no default selected' -- the type lie is acceptable because the Zod validation catches empty string on submit and shows the error message. No schema change needed.

issues_found.description: The discovery mentions 'Cards E+F+G' for the Horarios tab, but there is no separate Card G in ColaboradorDetalhe. There are only 6 sections: A (Info Pessoal), B (Contrato), C (Preferencias), E (Regras de Horario), F (Excecoes por Data), D (Excecoes). The 'Ciclo domingo + Folga fixa' section is PART of Card E, not a separate card. Tab mapping should be: Horarios = Cards E + F (not E+F+G).
issues_found.fix_required: Correct the tab mapping reference in subtask-3-1: Horarios tab contains Cards E (Regras de Horario, L848-1044) + F (Excecoes por Data, L1046-1108). There is no Card G.

critique.summary: The implementation plan is solid and well-structured. The critical discovery about HashRouter vs data router was correctly identified and the createHashRouter migration (phase-0) is the right approach -- I verified that createHashRouter and useBlocker are both available and properly typed in the installed react-router v7.13.0. The Blocker API (state/reset/proceed) matches the plan's assumptions exactly. Line numbers in ColaboradorDetalhe are accurate. The main concern is a medium-severity structural issue with the Form wrapper placement when restructuring into Tabs (must wrap Tabs, not live inside a single TabsContent), and a minor Toaster placement omission in the router migration. Both are easy to address during implementation. No high-severity issues found.
recommendations: Phase-0 router migration: import createHashRouter and RouterProvider from 'react-router-dom' (not 'react-router') to match existing import patterns

Phase-3 subtask-3-1: Place <Form {...colabForm}> OUTSIDE and WRAPPING the <Tabs> component, not inside TabsContent. Cards E/F/D will be inside Form but won't contain FormField elements (this is fine, Form is just a context provider)
Phase-0 subtask-0-2: Don't forget <Toaster /> placement -- keep it as sibling to RouterProvider inside TooltipProvider
For sexo default fix, use the simple cast approach: sexo: '' as 'M' | 'F' in defaultValues. The Zod schema catches the empty value on submit.
Consider running 'npm run dev' after phase-0 completes and manually testing ALL routes before proceeding to phases 1-3, since router migration is the highest-risk change
